wety: Literally “southern frozen ocean”, from بَحْر (bahr, “sea, ocean”) + مُنْجَمِد (munjamid, “frozen, congealed”) + جُنُوبی (junūbī, “south”), a partial calque of Persian اقیانوس منجمد جنوبی (oqyânus-e monjamed-e janubi).

def: Arctic Ocean
wety: Literally “northern frozen ocean”, from بَحْر (bahr, “sea, ocean”) + مُنْجَمِد (munjamid, “frozen, congealed”) + شُمالی (śumālī, “north”), a partial calque of Persian اقیانوس منجمد شمالی (oqyânus-e monjamed-e šomâli).

def: adj. Running; flowing. Bahtā pānī nir-malā, baṅdhā gaṅdīlā hoë, Jogī jan ramtā bhalā, dāg na lāge koë . Dohā. The running water's pure, the stagnant water, foul, The wandering jogi's pure, nostain pollutes his soul. bahte pānī meṅ hāth dhonā , v. n. lit . To wash one's hands in the running stream. To do what one has to do in one's life time; to make hay while the sun shines; to swim with the tide. Bahte pānī meṅ jis kā jī chāhe hāth dho le . Prov. All who will may wash in the running stream. (Make hay while the sun shines).
more: part. act. Running (water), afloat. بـﮩتي پاني مين هاتهـ دهونا bahte pānī meṅ hāth dhonā, To pay attention to oneʼs friends, or to attend to oneʼs own duty or affairs while the opportunity is favourable; to make hay while the sun shines.

subs: lit. 'To wash one's hands in the running stream'; to pay attention to one's friends; to attend to one's own business or affairs while the opportunity is favourable, to make hay while the sun shines; to swim with the tide.

def: n. f. S. वह् to carry, to marry. 1. Wife; a son's wife; daughter-in-law; a bride. 1. Ghī saṅvāre kām, baṛī bahū kā nām . Prov. The flavour from the butter came, the elder daughter-in-law yet gets the name. 2. Jabtak bahū kuārī, tab tak sās wārī, Bahū āī god meṅ, lāḍ gayā haud meṅ . Prov. A maid unwed, the mother-in-law to be devoted see, As daughter-in-law embraced, her love and fondness out will flee. 3. Jān līyo bas ham ne, bahūaṛ, Tū bhī khūb pichhāne sai, Jaisī terī hotī aī vaisā hamnū jāne sai! W. Rus. Song. Yes, daughter-in-law, I know you now, Your penetration's great, I vow, As you've been bred you take me so! 4. Sās kahe 'merī āī bohaṛyā, jī apne ko wārūṅgī,' 'Tū hī, sāsū, pānī bhar lā, to se hī piswāūṅgī .' Rus. Wom. Song. The mother-in-law says, 'now a daughter-in-law I've got, To her my life I would devote.' Says she 'now ma, you shall go fill the water pot, And grind the corn for us, I vote.' 2. A boy's penis. bahū beṭī,—beṭyāṅ , n. f. 1. Wives and daughters. 2. Ladies; gentlewomen. Bahū, beṭī sab rakhte haiṅ . Prov. saying. All have wives and daughters. (Said to one who casts glances at another's female relatives).
more: s. f. 1. A daughter-in-law, a sonʼs wife. 2. A wife.

A relative or adjective compound (a class of compounds in which the last member, being a substantive, loses its independence as well as its original grammatical character, and together with the first member serves only to qualify or define another word; the words so formed are usually called epithets; e.g. ćatur-bhuj, 'the four-armed,' an epithet of Vishnu. The development from a noun to an adjective was influenced by Prakrit 𑀅𑀳𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (pahutta-), from Sanskrit प्रभूत (prabhūta, “come forth; abundant”). Compare Bengali বহুত (bohut), Gujarati બહુ (bahu…

def: adj. = bahut, q.v. s.m. & adv. more: n. m. 1. An evil spirit; demon; fiend; goblin. A malignant spirit haunting cemeteries, lurking in trees, animating carcases, and deluding or devouring human beings. Bharmā bhūt, sankā ḍāin . Rus. Prov. Our vain imaginations are the devils, and our fears the ogres which affright. 2. An ugly looking or hideous being. 3. A very wicked, or vicious person. 4. One of the elements. See tat . 5. ( Gram .) The preterite or past tense ( bhūt kāl ). bhūt utār'nā, yā jalānā , v. a. To destroy the evil influence of an evil spirit; to exorcise. bhūt bannā , v. n. To be intoxicated; to be a devil. Paṛā rahe bhūt banā, jānoṅ koī paret ne is ko gherā, Nahīṅ chhoṛe woh sabzī pīnā, maiṅ samjhāë bauhterā . Song. Lāonī . Māṅ sūṅ kākī bheṛ rakhe saë, ghar nyārā kar le bāppū, Yo maiṅ sīkh det sūṅ āchhī, tauṅ hirde dhar le bāppū . Rus. Song. 3. Hāth kā denā, aur bair bisānā . Prov. Who money lends oft loses friend…
s. m. Enmity, hostility, animosity, revenge. بيرلينا bair lenā, v. a. To revenge.

The saint his saintship doffs, with sin his soul attaints, In greed and lust all sunk, both perfect men and saints.
s. m. 1. The religious ascetick, or he who abandons terrestrial objects, thoughts, passions, &c. 2. A kind of wandering faḳīr who practices certain austerities.

def: s.f. A woman (in general)
more: Rus. n. f. S. वरवर्णिनी A woman. See istrī. bīr-bandhnī , n. f. A silver or gold chain worn by women on the forehead.
subs: s.f. Woman, wife ( = bīr-bānī, q.v.s.v. bīr).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: adj. Clear, lucid, evident, manifest.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀬𑀡 (vayaṇa) Sanskrit वचन (vacana).

def: s.m. A mark fixed to public writings by the magistrate or any principal officer.
more: s. m. A mark fixed to publick writings by the magistrate or any principal officer.

def: s.m. Egg; testicle.
more: n. m. بيض he made it white. 1. An egg. 2. A testicle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیضه (bayza), from Arabic بَيْضَة (bayḍa).

ety: fem. of abyaẓ; rt. بيض 'to surpass in whiteness'
def: adj. & s.m. White; bright, clear, clean, pure; — the sun
more: 1. adj. White. 2. s. m. The Sun. يدبيضادکهانا yad-i-baiẓā dikhānā, v. a. To distinguish oneʼs self in any work (an allusion taken from the luminous hand of Moses).
subs: To distinguish oneself in any work; to do wonders, perform a miracle (in allusion to the white hand of Moses).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بَيْضَاء (bayḍāʔ), feminine singular of أَبْيَض (ʔabyaḍ).

def: s.m. The perquisite of the magistrate, &c. for marking public papers (see baiẓ).
more: s. m. The perquisite of the magistrate, &c. for marking publick papers.

def: s. f. The name of a book containing a commentary on the ḳurʼān.
adj. Fashioned in the shape of an egg, egg-shaped, oval (syn. anḍākār); relating to eggs; made of eggs.
more: (from بيضه) adj. Fashioned in the shape of an egg. خط بيضوي ḵẖat̤t̤-i-baiẓawī, s. m. A form of the Persian Nastaﻌlīḳ character, in which the curved tails of the letters are segments of an oval: opposed to خط آفتابي ḵẖat̤t̤-i-āftābī, in which they are portions of a circle.

def: s. m. Bill or deed of sale.
Far from home, foreigner.
s.m.f. (?), Tribute, tax, toll, duty, impost, cess
more: n. m. Old. P. bāz . Used only in law. Duty; cess; impost; tax; tribute.
Poet. n. m. S. बाजिः A horse. Raghu-nandan chandan khaur dīye, gaj, bāj nachāvat āvat haiṅ . Bhajan. With sandal-painted brow and stately elephant and prancing horse, Ramchandar comes.
s. m. f. A tax, duty, toll, impost, &c. tribute. باجدار bāj-dār, A tax-gatherer, a collector of tribute or revenue. باج گذار bāj-guzār, One who pays taxes, duties, &c.
(perhaps for Pers. باز Back, &c.) postpos. In the separation or absence, besides. Walī says, تجهـ باج جان ودل کون نشاط وطرب نهين In thy absence there is neither joy nor mirth for my soul and heart.
subs: bāj-gīr, s.m. Collector of tribute or revenue; tax-gatherer
bāj-guzār, s.m. A tributary (king, &c.); feudatory or dependent state; one who pays taxes, duties, &c.

def: prep. Without, in the absence of ( = ba-juz. — The word is used by the poet Walī, and would appear to be peculiar to Southern India).
s.m. Horse ( = bājī, q.v.).
s.m. Hawk ( = bāz, of which it is a corruption).
(see بجانا) v. n. To effervesce with noise in putrefying.
s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
more: n. m. S. वाद्य from वद् to sound. A musical instrument; instrumental music; music. bājā bajānā , v. a. To play on a musical instrument; to pipe, fiddle, thrum, etc; to strike up (music). bājā gājā ; Rus. bāj gāj , n. m. 1. Various musical instruments. 2. The sound or clangor of various musical instruments. Baṛe bāje gāje se barāt niklī .
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀚𑁆𑀚 (vajja), from Sanskrit वाद्य (vādya), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *wā́dyam, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *wā́dyam.

def: adj. In place; proper, suitable, fit, becoming; right, just, true; — adv. Fitly, properly, rightly, as it should be all right; just so, precisely; (ironically) just so, very true, very like a whale; — ba-jāʼe, prep. In place (of, -ke), in lieu of, instead of
more: adj. P. ba in, jā place. 1. Just ( ṭhīk ); true ( sach ); right; valid. Bajā kahe jise ālam, use bajā samjho, Zabān-i-khalq ko naqqāraë Khudā samjho . Zauq. Whate'er the world calls good do you good deem, The people's voice the voice of heaven esteem. 2. Conformable to fact; proper; suitable; acceptable. 3. Fair; fit; becoming. 4. ( Ironic .) Nonsensical; absurd; very like a whale. * Biṭhā dīyā (A witticism). 1. Brought, or made to fit in his verse. 2. Placed on the throne (the king regnant). bajā , adv. In place; fitly; rightly; properly; all right; as it should be.
In place; fit, opportune, right, proper, accurate, true, properly, fitly.
subs: s.f. Performance, accomplishment, fulfilment, execution, discharge
v.t. To perform, accomplish, fulfil, execute, discharge, carry out (an order, &c.), give effect to; to comply with, obey ( = bajānā).

def: s. m. See باجا bājā.
A band of musick ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
v.t. See s.v. ba-jā. s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
more: v. a. 1. To discharge; execute; perform; fulfil. Phr.—To carry into execution; do one's bidding. Jo kuchh farmāoge bajā lāūṅgā . 2. To give effect to; work out; achieve. 3. To comply with; obey; carry out. 4. To bring to pass; bring or carry through. hukm bajā lānā , v. a. To obey an order.
v. n. To perform, accomplish, execute, effect, to obey.
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).

def: n. f. Execution; performance; discharge; accomplishment. bajā-āwurī-i-ahkām . Performance or discharge of a duty. bajā-āwurī-i-hukm, yā khidmat . Execution of duty.
adv. By force or violence, forcibly, violently ( = biʼl-jabr, and jabr-se).
instead, alternatively
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بجای (ba-jāy).

def: prep. See s.v. ba-jā. s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
more: adv. Instead, in the place of.
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).

def: adv. Instead; in the place of; in lieu of.
s.m. A species of serpent.
more: s. m. A kind of serpent.

def: adj. Still, quiet; lying still (through fear or fatigue); crouching, contracting oneself; delaying.
adv. With (one's) whole soul, heartily, cheerfully; — in or to the soul; at or to the point of death; in great straits, in great distress, in the utmost perplexity
more: Heartily, with, in or to soul. بجان لانا ba-jān lānā, To tease, tire, disgust.
subs: v.n. To be in imminent danger; to be in the utmost distress or perplexity; to be greatly harassed, plagued, annoyed, vexed, &c.
v.t. To worry to death, to plague, harass, annoy exceedingly, worry, tease, vex, disgust.

def: (see باجا) s. m. plur. Musical instruments.
s.m. Instruments of music
more: s. m. plur. Musical instruments. See باجا bājā.
s. Sounding, to sound: see باجنا bājnā.

ety: S. वादयत, rt. वद्, or वाद्य+H. caus. aff. ānā
def: v.t. To cause to sound, to sound; to play on (a musical instrument), to perform or execute music; to beat (a drum, gong, &c.); to cause to ring or resound; to try the ring of (a vessel, coin, &c. in order to ascertain its soundness); to put in action, set agoing, ply (e.g. jūte bajānā)
subs: s.m. Player on a musical instrument, musician; minstrel (syn. bājantrī).

def: v.t. To execute, &c. = ba-jā lānā, q.v. s.m. Musical instrument; instrumental music, music
more: v. a. S. वद् to sound. 1. To play upon a musical instrument, etc. 1. Gurūr-i-husn par ham se woh nāhaq jhāṅj kartā hai, Yeh naubat chand-rozah hai, bajāë jiskā jī chāhe . Kāmilā Begam. In pride of beauty she all wilful scolds me still, This rule short lived's a kettledrum played on at will. 2. Āh! aṅdesā mat karo, kārī āyo ghāo, Jab-lag jīu hai aṅg meṅ, tab-lag bīn bajāo . Dohā. Ah me! the fatal dart hath struck, but heed me not, While yet this heart doth beat Oh let me hear thy lute! ☞ Addressed to his enchantress by the swan pierced by a dart from the jealous husband. 3. Din bhar baiṭhā huqqā bajāyā kartā hai . He sits all day and plays on his hubble-bubble. 2. To produce any sound; to drone. 3. To examine; test; test the purity of a coin by the sound ( parakhnā ). Ṭhok bajā-ke le lo . 4. To do or perform; execute; carry out. Gale paṛe bajāë sidh . Prov. Whate'er devolves on you 'tis f…
v. a. To sound, to play upon an instrument of musick, to perform or execute (musick), to beat (a drum, &c.).
subs: To play on a musical instrument; to strike up (music)
bājā-bajantar, s.m. Band of musicians, or of music ( = bajantarī)
s.m. Various musical in struments; sound or clanguor of many and various musical instruments (also bāj-gāj)
s.m. Maker or vendor of musical instruments; player on any instrument of music, musician ( = bājantarī).
